summary: Superb Dan Healy soundboard captures a tight 11/11/85 Meadowlands show with standout Scarlet‑Fire segway, Bird Song and a rare Walking The Dog.
review: The recording is a pristine Dan Healy cassette soundboard that delivers crystal‑clear mix and the classic mid‑80s punch. Musically the concert is tight from start to finish – the first set smokes with Bird Song and Looks Like Rain, while the second set shines on the legendary Scarlet > Fire transition, a rare Walking The Dog, and solid jams like Estimated Prophet into Eyes of the World, despite a slightly predictable ending.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high reviewer scores (mostly 4‑5 stars), strong praise for both performance and Dan Healy soundboard quality, with no significant complaints.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Dan Healy’s cassette mix is crisp and well‑balanced; reviewers note superb clarity and “Killer 85 Healy sound,” with only minor fade‑out tail at the very end of the second set.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
Estimated Prophet > Eyes of the World
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set fires from the opening with strong jams; second set is solid though a bit predictable and tails off slightly toward the end, but overall maintains high energy.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Minor lyric flub on Althea noted, but his lead work remains packed and impressive.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir is loud and clearly having a great time, adding extra energy to the performance.
